question.text: The extraordinary dividend threshold for adjustment in derivatives contracts is:

answer.text: Dividends at or above 2% of the market value of the underlying stock
Dividends at or above 5% of the market value of the underlying stock
Dividends at or above 10% of the face value of the share
All dividends trigger an adjustment in derivatives contracts

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Ordinary dividends (below 2% of market price) do not trigger any adjustment. Extraordinary dividends (≥ 2% of market price) cause strike price adjustments in option contracts. The market price for this purpose is the closing price on the day before dividend announcement. **The Trap:** The 2% threshold is of MARKET price, not face value. And only extraordinary dividends trigger adjustments — ordinary dividends do not. **Cross-Exam Context:** Corporate action adjustments in derivatives are tested in VIII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** < 2% market price = ordinary = no adjustment. ≥ 2% = extraordinary = strike price adjusted. Bonus/split always adjusted.
